text:
CT ABDOMEN AND PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST
SURVEILLANCE EXAMINATION

CLINICAL INDICATION: 53-year-old female with history of FIGO stage IIIC high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma, bilateral, with peritoneal carcinomatosis. Status post primary debulking surgery (TAH/BSO, omentectomy, peritoneal stripping, right hemidiaphragm peritonectomy) with R0 resection, followed by adjuvant carboplatin/paclitaxel x6 cycles. Currently on niraparib maintenance therapy (dose-reduced to 100 mg daily following prior Grade 3 thrombocytopenia). Surveillance CT approximately 6 months after chemotherapy completion / approximately 3 months following niraparib initiation. CA-125 9 U/mL (normalized).

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the abdomen and pelvis was performed following administration of intravenous iodinated contrast material in the portal venous phase. Oral contrast was also administered. Axial images were obtained with reconstructions in coronal and sagittal planes. Comparison is made to prior CT abdomen/pelvis from approximately 6 months ago (post-chemotherapy restaging examination).

FINDINGS:

Liver: The liver is normal in size and morphology. No focal hepatic lesions are identified. The hepatic parenchyma enhances homogeneously. No biliary ductal dilatation. The perihepatically, specifically along the hepatic dome where surface implants were previously noted at staging, there is no identifiable peritoneal thickening or nodularity. Appearance is clean and smooth.

Gallbladder and Biliary System: Gallbladder is present and unremarkable. No cholelithiasis. No biliary ductal dilation.

Spleen: Unremarkable in size and attenuation. No splenic lesions.

Pancreas: The pancreas is normal in contour and signal intensity throughout its head, body, and tail. No ductal dilatation. No peripancreatic stranding.

Adrenal Glands: Bilateral adrenal glands are normal in appearance without evidence of nodularity or enlargement.

Kidneys and Ureters: Both kidneys enhance symmetrically with no hydronephrosis, nephrolithiasis, or suspicious renal masses. Normal excretion of contrast bilaterally. Ureters not obstructed.

Bowel: Small and large bowel loops are normal in caliber without wall thickening, obstruction, or pneumatosis. No evidence of mesenteric infiltration or tethering. The mesentery appears clean. Post-surgical changes at the terminal ileum and right lower quadrant are unchanged from prior study; the appendix is absent consistent with prior appendectomy.

Peritoneum and Omentum: No peritoneal implants or nodular thickening identified throughout the peritoneal surfaces. The greater and lesser omentum, previously the site of bulky omental caking at diagnosis, appears entirely unremarkable with no soft tissue nodularity or infiltration. The bilateral paracolic gutters are clear. The right and left hemidiaphragm peritoneal surfaces are smooth without nodularity; the right hemidiaphragm peritoneum, which was surgically stripped, shows expected post-operative fibrous change but no suspicious nodularity or soft tissue thickening to suggest recurrent implants. The pelvic peritoneum is unremarkable.

Ascites: No free fluid or ascites identified within the abdomen or pelvis.

Pelvis: The uterus and bilateral ovaries are absent, consistent with prior total abdominal hysterectomy and bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy. The vaginal cuff appears intact without evidence of soft tissue thickening or nodularity to suggest cuff recurrence. The bladder wall is smooth and regular. No pelvic masses. No suspicious soft tissue lesions in the presacral space or lateral pelvic sidewalls.

Lymph Nodes: No enlarged lymph nodes in the retroperitoneum, along the aorta or inferior vena cava, iliac chains, inguinal regions, or pelvic sidewalls. The para-aortic and pelvic nodal chains appear clean.

Vascular Structures: Aorta and inferior vena cava are normal in caliber. No vascular abnormalities.

Bone and Soft Tissues: Visualized osseous structures demonstrate no lytic or sclerotic lesions suspicious for metastatic involvement. Soft tissues are unremarkable. Mild degenerative changes noted in the lumbar spine, age-appropriate.

Incidental Findings: Trace bilateral pleural thickening, unchanged and consistent with prior reactive changes noted on staging chest CT. No new pleural abnormality identified on the included lung bases.

IMPRESSION:

1. No peritoneal implants, omental nodularity, or ascites. The abdomen and pelvis are clear of any imaging findings to suggest active peritoneal carcinomatosis.

2. The hepatic dome and right hemidiaphragm peritoneum, previously sites of surface and diaphragmatic tumor involvement, appear clean without suspicious nodularity or recurrent soft tissue deposition.

3. No pelvic masses, vaginal cuff nodularity, or lymphadenopathy.

4. Post-surgical changes are stable and expected following prior debulking surgery.

5. Overall, the examination is reassuring and shows no imaging evidence of disease recurrence. Findings are consistent with ongoing complete remission on niraparib maintenance therapy.

Reporting radiologist: [Attending Radiologist Signature on File]
